body: Seed question for the first epistemic cycle.
title: What makes a research cycle improve itself?

body:
The best signal of research progress is whether concepts get reused across experiments. Source question: What makes a research cycle improve itself?

Formal statement: If concepts are reused in independent experiments, then future cycles need fewer new primitives.

Analysis: survived in toy simulation.

assumptions: cycle logs are complete
questions can be compared by type

title: Reusable concepts are stronger than surviving hypotheses #1

body: The hypothesis is useful only if it produces discriminating observations.

counterexamples: A hypothesis can survive because it is vague rather than because it is useful.
A bad abstraction can be reused often if it is too broad.
